title: Pringleochloa stolonifera
intro: Pringleochloa stolonifera is a species of grass native to Australia, belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, with its reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pringleochloa stolonifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pringleochloa stolonifera is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations.

The genus Pringleochloa is part of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is a group of grasses that are commonly found in warm and dry environments.
